entries.reasoning: Building on run 94730 (3 coils, QA) but switching to MUSE surface with 4 coils, maximum allowed force/torque thresholds, and baseline MUSE cc/cs to address the severe force/torque violations seen in all QA parent runs.
Using run 85897 (4 coils) as base, switching to MUSE with high force/torque limits and moderate engineering constraints to find feasible solutions on the new surface.
Exploring MUSE surface with 3 coils using baseline cc/cs thresholds but high force/torque limits which are universally violated in QA parent runs.
Mutating run 81206 (4 coils) to MUSE surface with high force/torque limits and relaxed cc but tighter cs to establish feasibility baseline on new surface.
Using baseline MUSE thresholds with 5 coils to explore if more coils help distribute forces better while maintaining reasonable engineering limits.
Building on run 82267 with tight cc margins, switching to MUSE with 4 coils instead of 3, maximum allowed force/torque limits, and minimum cc threshold to test feasibility.
Exploring maximum coil count (6) on MUSE to see if more coils can distribute forces better and achieve feasibility with moderate force/torque thresholds.
Mutating run 85472 to MUSE with 4 coils instead of 3, relaxing force/torque significantly while keeping other constraints moderate to balance feasibility and performance.
Conservative exploration with very relaxed constraints across all dimensions to establish baseline feasibility on MUSE surface before tightening in future batches.
Using run 81167 as base, switching to MUSE with moderately high force/torque limits and near-baseline MUSE thresholds to test if the MUSE surface geometry naturally produces lower forces than QA.
Using baseline MUSE thresholds with maximum allowed force/torque limits to establish feasibility on MUSE surface, addressing the severe force/torque violations seen in all QA parent runs.
Building on run 85897 (4 coils) but switching to MUSE with high force/torque limits and relaxed cc/cs to find feasible solutions on the new surface.
Exploring 3 coils on MUSE with very relaxed constraints across all dimensions to establish baseline feasibility before tightening in future batches.
Mutating run 94730 (3 coils) to MUSE with 4 coils and maximum allowed force/torque thresholds plus near-baseline MUSE cc/cs to address the severe violations.
Using baseline MUSE thresholds with 5 coils to explore if more coils help distribute forces better while maintaining reasonable engineering limits.
Mutating run 81206 (4 coils) to MUSE surface with high force/torque limits and relaxed cc/cs to establish feasibility baseline on new surface.
Exploring maximum coil count (6) on MUSE to see if more coils can distribute forces better and achieve feasibility with moderate force/torque thresholds.

Using run 85897 (4 coils) as base, switching to MUSE with high force/torque limits and relaxed engineering constraints to find feasible solutions on the new surface.
Exploring MUSE surface with 3 coils using baseline cc/cs thresholds but maximum force/torque limits which are universally violated in QA parent runs.
Mutating run 81206 (4 coils) to MUSE surface with high force/torque limits and moderate cc/cs to establish feasibility baseline on new surface.
Using baseline MUSE thresholds with 5 coils to explore if more coils help distribute forces better while maintaining reasonable engineering limits.
Building on run 82267 with tight cc margins, switching to MUSE with 4 coils instead of 3, maximum allowed force/torque limits, and minimum cc threshold to test feasibility.
Exploring maximum coil count (6) on MUSE to see if more coils can distribute forces better and achieve feasibility with moderate force/torque thresholds.
Mutating run 85472 to MUSE with 4 coils instead of 3, relaxing force/torque significantly while keeping other constraints moderate to balance feasibility and performance.
Conservative exploration with very relaxed constraints across all dimensions to establish baseline feasibility on MUSE surface before tightening in future batches.
Using run 81167 as base, switching to MUSE with moderately high force/torque limits and near-baseline MUSE thresholds to test if the MUSE surface geometry naturally produces lower forces than QA.

Starting with baseline MUSE reference case but using maximum allowed force/torque thresholds to address the severe violations seen in all QA parent runs. This establishes a feasibility baseline on MUSE surface.
Building on run 85897 (4 coils) which had the smallest force violations among 4-coil parents. Switching to MUSE with high but not maximum force/torque limits and moderate engineering constraints.
Exploring 3 coils on MUSE with very relaxed constraints across all dimensions to establish if fewer coils can achieve feasibility with the new surface geometry.
Mutating the best-performing 3-coil parent to MUSE with 4 coils and maximum force/torque limits. The parent had tight cc margins, so using relaxed cc threshold on new surface.
Testing if 5 coils can distribute forces better and achieve feasibility with more moderate force/torque thresholds while maintaining near-baseline engineering constraints.
Using run 81206 (4 coils) as base since it had moderate force violations. Switching to MUSE with high force/torque limits and relaxed but reasonable engineering constraints.
Exploring maximum coil count to see if 6 coils can distribute forces well enough to achieve feasibility with more aggressive force/torque thresholds while using tight engineering constraints.
Building on run 82267 which had very tight cc margins. Switching to MUSE with 4 coils instead of 3, near-maximum force/torque limits, and baseline-level engineering constraints.

Testing if MUSE surface geometry naturally produces lower forces by using moderate force/torque thresholds with very relaxed engineering constraints to see if we can achieve both force feasibility and good coil quality.
Mutating run 85472 to MUSE with 4 coils instead of 3, high force/torque limits, and relaxed engineering constraints to balance feasibility with performance on the new surface.
Building on the best 3-coil QA parent but switching to MUSE surface with 4 coils and maximum allowed force/torque thresholds to address the severe violations. Using baseline MUSE cc/cs thresholds.
Using the 4-coil parent with smallest force violations as base. Switching to MUSE with high but not maximum force/torque limits and relaxed engineering constraints to find feasible solutions.
Exploring 3 coils on MUSE with maximum force/torque limits and relaxed engineering constraints to establish if fewer coils can achieve feasibility with the new surface geometry.

Testing if 5 coils can distribute forces better and achieve feasibility with more moderate force/torque thresholds while maintaining reasonable engineering constraints.
